exercises.title: Trace ArrayList basics
hints: Still has 2 elements → not empty → false

exercises.title: Declare and populate ArrayList
exercises.instructions: Fill in the blanks to create, populate, and iterate an ArrayList.

exercises.title: Numbers list — sum and max
exercises.scenario: Build numeric ArrayList and compute stats.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `List<Integer>` with these scores: 85, 92, 78, 95, 88.
Compute and print the sum and the maximum.

Expected output:
Sum: 438
Max: 95
hints: List<Integer> with autoboxing (add int, stored as Integer)

exercises.title: Fix the List<int> error
exercises.scenario: Primitives can't be generic type parameters.
exercises.instructions:
This code doesn't compile. Fix it using the correct type.

Expected output:
[10, 20, 30]

bugs.issue: List<int> — primitives cannot be type parameters. Must use Integer.

hints: Primitives (int, double, boolean) cannot be generic type arguments
Use wrapper class: int → Integer, double → Double, boolean → Boolean
Java autoboxes: numbers.add(10) works even though 10 is an int literal

exercises.title: Filter strings into new list
exercises.instructions:
Given a list of city names, create a new list containing only cities with more than 7 characters.

Input: Hyderabad, Mumbai, Bengaluru, Pune, Chennai, Delhi

Expected output:
[Hyderabad, Bengaluru]
hints: Create source list with all cities
Loop source, check length > 7, add to result

exercises.title: ArrayList of objects
exercises.scenario: Store and iterate objects in ArrayList.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `Point` class with x (int) and y (int). Store 4 points in a `List<Point>`. Print each point and the one closest to origin (smallest distance √(x²+y²)).

Expected output:
(0,3)
(2,1)
(4,4)
(1,5)
Closest to origin: (2,1)
hints: Track closest: initialize with first point, update if smaller distance found

exercises.title: Replace manual array with ArrayList
hints: Replace String[] + int count with List<String>

exercises.title: Fix ConcurrentModificationException
exercises.scenario: Can't remove while iterating with for-each.
exercises.instructions:
This code throws ConcurrentModificationException. Fix it.

Expected output:
[Priya, Vikram]

bugs.issue: Removing from list while iterating with for-each causes ConcurrentModificationException

hints: Cannot modify list in for-each loop body
Or iterate backwards by index: for (int i = size-1; i >= 0; i--)

exercises.title: Sort ArrayList of objects
exercises.scenario: Sort list by a field using Comparator.
exercises.instructions:
Create a `List<Student>` (name, gpa). Sort by GPA DESCENDING. Print sorted list.

Expected output:
Aisha (3.95)
Priya (3.80)
Raj (3.75)
Vikram (3.50)
hints: Anonymous class or lambda both work

exercises.title: Deduplicate ArrayList
exercises.instructions:
Write a method `removeDuplicates(List<String> list)` that returns a new list with duplicates removed, preserving first-occurrence order.

Input: ["apple", "banana", "apple", "cherry", "banana", "date"]
Expected output:
[apple, banana, cherry, date]
hints: For each item: only add if !result.contains(item)
contains() uses equals() — works for Strings
Order is preserved: items added in first-seen order

exercises.title: Shopping cart with ArrayList
exercises.scenario: Practical domain — cart operations on object list.
exercises.instructions:
Create `CartItem` (productName, price, qty). Build a `ShoppingCart` using `List<CartItem>`:
- `addItem(CartItem)` — adds item
- `removeByName(String)` — removes first item with that name
- `totalPrice()` — sums price × qty for all items
- `printReceipt()` — prints each item and total

Expected output:
Wireless Mouse x1 = ₹719.1
Keyboard x2 = ₹3998.0
USB Hub x3 = ₹1497.0
TOTAL: ₹6214.1

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Wireless Mouse x1 = ₹719.1
Keyboard x2 = ₹3998.0
USB Hub x3 = ₹1497.0
TOTAL: ₹6214.1

hints: totalPrice: sum lineTotal() for each item

exercises.title: Trace ArrayList remove overloads
exercises.scenario: remove(int) vs remove(Object) on Integer list.
hints: remove(1): removes element at INDEX 1 → removes 20 → [10, 30, 40]
This is the classic remove ambiguity with Integer lists

exercises.title: Contact list manager
exercises.scenario: Full ArrayList class with add, remove, search, sort.
exercises.instructions:
Build a `ContactManager` using `List<Contact>` (name, phone, email):
- `add(Contact)` — validates non-empty name
- `removeByEmail(String)` — remove by email
- `findByName(String)` — case-insensitive search, returns list of matches
- `sortByName()` — sort alphabetically
- `countByDomain(String)` — count contacts with email at given domain (after @)

Test with 5 contacts, remove one, find by partial name, count gmail.com.

Expected output:
Found 2 with 'ra': Raj Kumar, Priya Rao
gmail.com contacts: 2

test_cases.expected_stdout:
Found 2 with 'ra': Raj Kumar, Priya Rao
gmail.com contacts: 2

hints: countByDomain: indexOf('@') gives the split point

exercises.title: Grade histogram from ArrayList
exercises.instructions:
Given `List<Integer> scores`, write:
- `average(List<Integer>)` → double average
- `countAbove(List<Integer>, int threshold)` → count scores > threshold  
- `topN(List<Integer>, int n)` → returns new sorted List<Integer> of top N scores

Test with: [72, 88, 91, 65, 78, 95, 83, 67, 91, 79]

Expected output:
Average: 80.9
Above 80: 4
Top 3: [95, 91, 91]
hints: average: sum all, divide by size(). Cast to double.
countAbove: loop and count where s > threshold
topN: copy list, sort descending, return subList(0, n)
new ArrayList<>(scores) creates a COPY of the list

exercises.title: Polymorphic notification queue
exercises.scenario: ArrayList of abstract parent type — Module 5 meets Module 6.
exercises.instructions:
Create abstract class `Notification` with abstract `deliver()` and `getPriority()` (int). Create `EmailNotification`, `SMSNotification`, `PushNotification` (each with different priority and delivery message).

Build a `NotificationQueue` using `List<Notification>` (abstract parent):
- `enqueue(Notification)` adds to list
- `deliverAll()` sorts by priority DESCENDING, then calls deliver() on each
- `countByType(String type)` — counts using instanceof check

Expected output:
[PUSH-P1] App update available
[SMS-P2] Your OTP is 123456
[EMAIL-P3] Welcome to CodeQuest!
Push: 1, SMS: 1, Email: 1

test_cases.expected_stdout:
[PUSH-P1] App update available
[SMS-P2] Your OTP is 123456
[EMAIL-P3] Welcome to CodeQuest!
Push: 1, SMS: 1, Email: 1

hints: Sort by priority ascending: Integer.compare(a.getPriority(), b.getPriority())
deliver() is polymorphic — each subclass's version runs
countByType uses instanceof — appropriate here for type-specific counting
